jokes.text: How many of you children have gone out to your car and found a flyer on the windshield? Are you the guy that puts them there? No. I'm his assistant. But one time he was sick and he let me do it. I totally screwed it up.
jokes.setup: Kids expect to hear about an exciting job, Kirk builds up to being the flyer guy
jokes.punchline: He's just the assistant and even screwed that up once
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Kirk's pathetic career trajectory delivered with escalating disappointment. Classic cringe comedy structure.

jokes.text: That's it. I see. Well, we still have 56 minutes left. Any questions?
jokes.setup: Career day speaker finishes immediately
jokes.punchline: Teacher's dead acceptance of having almost an hour to kill
jokes.explanation: Good awkward teacher moment - resigned to disaster

jokes.text: Do you know Mom's getting re-married? What? But she... I think we should probably talk about that later, Son. No. You might as well talk about it now.
jokes.setup: Kirk is already having a bad Career Day presentation
jokes.punchline: His son casually destroys him with news about his ex-wife in front of the class
jokes.explanation: Brutal character comedy that perfectly captures Kirk's pathetic life and Milhouse's oblivious cruelty. Excellent escalation of cringe.

jokes.text: I can't stare at those lifeless fisheyes anymore!
jokes.setup: Skinner is complaining about having to go back to teaching
jokes.punchline: His description of students as having 'lifeless fisheyes'
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific line that captures Skinner's disdain for his students in a vivid, quotable way.

jokes.text: Yes. Seamus. Ninety minutes of watching a man drink in a bathtub.
jokes.setup: Mention of previous bad speakers
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of a 90-minute presentation of a man drinking in a bathtub
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and economical - paints a complete ridiculous picture in just a few words.

jokes.text: What? This is your doing, Willie! I'll turn your groin to pudding! You speak like a poet, but you punch like one, too!
jokes.setup: Seamus threatens Willie with violence
jokes.punchline: Willie's comeback about punching like a poet (weak)
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay setup/punchline combo, but fairly predictable structure.

jokes.text: We have a special this month. Mr. Blackwell debates Mr. T. Oh, please. I've seen nicer chains on a set of snow tires. I pity the fool who derives self-esteem from mocking other people's clothes. I hate myself.
jokes.setup: Famous fashion critic vs. famous chain-wearing celebrity
jokes.punchline: Both deliver their signature lines, then Mr. T's existential crisis
jokes.explanation: Great concept - putting these two celebrities together and having Mr. T's self-awareness create comedy.

jokes.text: I pity the fool who derives self-esteem from mocking other people's clothes. I hate myself.
jokes.setup: Mr. T's typical 'pity the fool' catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Makes profound point, Blackwell immediately admits guilt
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion - Mr. T wins moral argument, opponent crumbles

jokes.text: And in a gutless act of political correctness, Pizza Day will now be known as Italian-American Sauce Bread Day. Now that's sensitivity. Right, Giuseppe? Giuseppe is such a happy monkey.
jokes.setup: Announcement about politically correct pizza day name change
jokes.punchline: Immediately calls the Italian janitor a monkey
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic structure - preaching sensitivity then immediately being insensitive. Perfect Skinner character moment.

jokes.text: I hope this is important. I've got a hot date tonight. With Sarah Jessica Barker.
jokes.setup: Danger Dog mentions having a hot date
jokes.punchline: It's with Sarah Jessica Parker as a dog (Barker)
jokes.explanation: Basic celebrity name pun. Functional but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: The mayor's barf is worse than his bite.
jokes.setup: Response to puke ray threat
jokes.punchline: Variation on 'bark is worse than bite' with barf
jokes.explanation: Predictable pun on common phrase. Low-effort wordplay.

jokes.text: Le mot juste.
jokes.setup: Following a terrible pun
jokes.punchline: Pretentious French phrase meaning 'exactly the right word'
jokes.explanation: Absurd pretension after a bad pun creates unexpected comedy through contrast.

jokes.text: If you freeze the frame, you can see that the chunks of barf are actually pictures of our animators and their friends.
jokes.setup: Behind-the-scenes animation trivia
jokes.punchline: The disgusting detail that animators put their faces in the vomit
jokes.explanation: Great meta-joke about animation industry practices, with gross-out humor element.

jokes.text: Why does Danger Dog mean more to me than school or church? Because those things suck.
jokes.setup: Innocent child's question about cartoon's meaning
jokes.punchline: Creator's blunt dismissal of education and religion
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from inappropriate adult response to child, economically delivered.

jokes.text: Are you kidding? This is the easiest job in the world. I spend most of my time eating candy and going to R-rated movies.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks about hard work in animation
jokes.punchline: Creator admits it's easy and he just goofs off
jokes.explanation: Decent subversion of expected 'hard work' message, though somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Kids, all I ever did was sit in class and doodle. Like this one. It's what your principal would look like as a woman.
jokes.setup: Admitting he just doodled in school
jokes.punchline: Shows drawing of Skinner as a woman
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that would be funny to see, plus the inappropriate nature of the drawing.

jokes.text: I won't stand by while you're glamorizing sass.
jokes.setup: Skinner's reason for pulling the fire alarm
jokes.punchline: He's against 'glamorizing sass' - absurdly dramatic concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner line - bureaucratic language applied to ridiculous concern. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: I'm called Ralph.
jokes.setup: Kids are naming their cartoon characters with variations on 'Danger'
jokes.punchline: Ralph just states his own name as if it's a character name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph Wiggum moment - completely misses the assignment but delivers it with such innocence it's hilarious.

jokes.text: My heart is pounding like Thor's hammer on Dr. Doom's titanium-infused faceplate!
jokes.setup: Stan Lee expressing excitement
jokes.punchline: Overly complex comic book metaphor for simple emotion
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing Stan Lee's tendency to over-complicate simple emotions with comic book references.

jokes.text: Hey, aren't you the guy who was stalking Lynda Carter?
jokes.setup: Meeting Stan Lee
jokes.punchline: Immediately brings up stalking allegations
jokes.explanation: Good Comic Book Guy moment - inappropriate knowledge

jokes.text: The term is 'courting.' The restraining order says, 'No, no.' But her eyes say, 'Yes, yes.'
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy mentions Stan Lee stalking Lynda Carter
jokes.punchline: Stan Lee's delusional justification and misreading of restraining order
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about stalking made funny through the character's complete delusion. Well-structured setup/punchline.

jokes.text: Did I say 'Spidey'? I meant stinky. 'Nuff said.
jokes.setup: Claims Spidey sense is tingling about comic
jokes.punchline: Corrects to 'stinky sense' - cruel putdown
jokes.explanation: Clever fake-out using his own character references

jokes.text: My Spidey sense is tingling. It's that good? Did I say 'Spidey'? I meant stinky. 'Nuff said.
jokes.setup: Stan Lee seems to praise Bart's comic with Spider-Man reference
jokes.punchline: Reveals he meant 'stinky' not 'Spidey' - it's terrible
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke that uses Stan Lee's speech patterns to set up the reversal. The 'Nuff said' catchphrase adds authenticity.

jokes.text: Stan Lee insulted me. But in Bizarro World, that means he likes me.
jokes.setup: Bart dealing with Stan Lee's harsh criticism
jokes.punchline: References comic book concept of Bizarro World to cope
jokes.explanation: Nice comic book reference used for emotional coping mechanism. Shows Bart's resilience through nerd culture.

jokes.text: I like Little Dot. Could you rip that off? I mean, who's gonna know? I'd know, Mom!
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting Bart copy an obscure comic character
jokes.punchline: Bart's unexpected integrity about plagiarism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart has more integrity than his mother about artistic theft. Role reversal comedy.

jokes.text: That little girl sure loved dots.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Little Dot character
jokes.punchline: Describes comic's obvious gimmick matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Mild Marge observation - understated way to describe character

jokes.text: Homer fighting with a lawn chair, getting angry and yelling 'D'oh!' while Bart watches and draws
jokes.setup: Bart needs inspiration for an angry character
jokes.punchline: Homer's ridiculous battle with inanimate furniture provides perfect model
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer physical comedy that perfectly sets up the 'Angry Dad' concept. Great visual gag with character integration.

jokes.text: This'll teach that stupid chair! I'm on fire!
jokes.setup: Homer attacking the chair in revenge
jokes.punchline: Declares victory while literally catching fire
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the chair fight with Homer's oblivious celebration while on fire.

jokes.text: Visual: Homer violently fighting with lawn chair, getting increasingly frustrated
jokes.setup: Homer tries to set up lawn chair
jokes.punchline: Chair defeats him in extended physical comedy sequence
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy setup. Works because it's relatable frustration taken to extreme.

jokes.text: I hope no one's drawing this!
jokes.setup: Homer in an embarrassing situation
jokes.punchline: Says exactly what Bart is doing, unaware he's being observed
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Homer says exactly what we know is happening. Meta-awareness adds comedy layer.

jokes.text: Oh, I've been there, man.
jokes.setup: Watching drunk dinosaurs on TV
jokes.punchline: Homer relates personally to intoxicated prehistoric animals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - relating to absurd TV content. Economical character comedy.

jokes.text: Oh, I've been there, man.
jokes.setup: Watching drunk dinosaur show
jokes.punchline: Homer relates to drunk dinosaur
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - empathizes with drunk dinosaur

jokes.text: Instead we bring you The Boring World of Niels Bohr.
jokes.setup: Canceling fun show
jokes.punchline: Replacement uses Bohr/Bore pun for physicist documentary
jokes.explanation: Clever physicist name pun - Bohr/boring works well

jokes.text: A couple of stink lines around his butt, and I'm done.
jokes.setup: Bart drawing angry Homer
jokes.punchline: Plans to add cartoon stink lines to dad's butt
jokes.explanation: Good cartoon visual reference - classic comic technique

jokes.text: 'Argh! I'm angry.' That's like something my dad would say.
jokes.setup: Martin reading Bart's comic
jokes.punchline: Relates to angry father figure
jokes.explanation: Universal father recognition - all dads are angry

jokes.text: It's a composite character. Your dad, my dad, a little of Maggie's dad. No. It's just Dad.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to claim Angry Dad isn't specifically Homer
jokes.punchline: Lisa points out Maggie's dad is also Homer, making the excuse absurd
jokes.explanation: Excellent logic joke that catches Bart in his weak excuse. Lisa's deadpan delivery and the absurdity of the composite character claim make this very strong.

jokes.text: Maybe Angry Dad needs a sidekick, Know-It-All Sister!
jokes.setup: Lisa wanting to be included
jokes.punchline: Creates character based on herself with self-aware name
jokes.explanation: Good self-awareness - Lisa knows her role in family dynamics

jokes.text: Your penciling is sub-Ziggy and the main character is off-model in every frame. However, I deem this rack-worthy.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy delivering harsh technical criticism
jokes.punchline: Despite all the flaws, he still approves it for the rack
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy character moment with technical jargon and condescending tone, but surprising acceptance.

jokes.text: Your penciling is sub-Ziggy and the main character is off-model in every frame. However, I deem this rack-worthy.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy reviewing Bart's comic harshly
jokes.punchline: Despite brutal criticism, still accepts it for store
jokes.explanation: Perfect CBG moment - pedantic criticism but low standards for sales

jokes.text: But only Batman fits in my Batmobile. Are you nuts? The Thing fits in there perfectly. Look. He's fitting right now.
jokes.setup: Child protects his Batman toy setup
jokes.punchline: Stan Lee forces incompatible action figure into Batmobile
jokes.explanation: Stan Lee's competitive need to promote Marvel over DC creates funny physical comedy with toys.

jokes.text: But only Batman fits in my Batmobile.
jokes.setup: Stan Lee suggesting different action figure
jokes.punchline: Kid has logical toy compatibility concern
jokes.explanation: Relatable kid logic - toy compatibility matters

jokes.text: You broke my Batmobile! Broke? Or made it better?
jokes.setup: Child upset about broken toy
jokes.punchline: Stan Lee's delusional reframing of obvious destruction
jokes.explanation: Stan Lee's refusal to admit fault creates comedy through obvious delusion. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: Stan Lee never left. And I'm starting to think that his mind is no longer in mint condition.
jokes.setup: Explaining Stan Lee's continued presence
jokes.punchline: Comic collecting terminology applied to mental state
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific joke using Comic Book Guy's obsession with mint condition items to describe mental decline. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Bin Laden in a Blender
jokes.setup: Showing other web cartoons
jokes.punchline: Extremely dark/crude cartoon concept
jokes.explanation: Shock humor typical of early Internet content

jokes.text: Well, it delivers what it promises.
jokes.setup: Watching 'Bin Laden in a Blender'
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact assessment of extremely crude content
jokes.explanation: Good understatement humor about lowbrow internet content. Bart's practical assessment adds comedy.

jokes.text: How is your company gonna make money? Do you have a business model?
jokes.setup: Lisa asking practical questions
jokes.punchline: Kid asking more sensible business questions than adults
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - practical questions adults ignore

jokes.text: How many shares of stock will it take to end this conversation? Two million. It is done.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking practical business questions
jokes.punchline: Executive's desperation to avoid answering leads to absurd stock offer
jokes.explanation: Good satire of dot-com bubble mentality - throwing stock at problems instead of answering basic questions.

jokes.text: Woo-hoo! When do I get paid? In 2012. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer excited about voice acting job
jokes.punchline: Payment delayed until distant future, classic Homer disappointment
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer emotional rollercoaster with signature catchphrase. Good internet startup satire timing.

jokes.text: Book that animal that always chomps on my groin. Susan Anton? No, the lemur!
jokes.setup: Executive trying to compete with internet content
jokes.punchline: Confusion between celebrity and groin-biting lemur
jokes.explanation: Absurd misdirection joke that creates mental image of Susan Anton biting groins. Good structure with the clarification.

jokes.text: I especially like his white shirt and blue pants.
jokes.setup: Moe commenting on Angry Dad cartoon
jokes.punchline: Describes Homer's actual outfit - doesn't realize it's him
jokes.explanation: Good visual irony - praises cartoon of person standing there

jokes.text: You're the Internet's number one non-porno site. Which makes you ten-trillionth overall.
jokes.setup: Lisa announcing Bart's internet success
jokes.punchline: Reality check about internet porn dominance
jokes.explanation: Excellent joke about early internet reality. Perfect setup/punchline structure that deflates excitement with truth.

jokes.text: We're just rehearsing The Motion Picture. How'd you like to be played by John Goodman? That's so obvious. It should be Gary Oldman.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his violent behavior as movie rehearsal
jokes.punchline: Debate over casting choices for Homer's biopic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's delusion and Marge's surprising sophistication about acting choices.

jokes.text: I'm a rage-aholic. I just can't live without rage-ahol!
jokes.setup: Homer admitting anger problem
jokes.punchline: Creates 'rage-ahol' addiction pun
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons wordplay - alcoholic becomes rage-aholic

jokes.text: I'm a rage-aholic. I just can't live without rage-ahol!
jokes.setup: Homer admitting his anger problem
jokes.punchline: Treating rage like alcoholism with made-up portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Good portmanteau wordplay that fits Homer's character. The made-up addiction term is memorable.

jokes.text: If you ask me, you should give up fatty foods. I said anger!
jokes.setup: Homer declaring he's giving up anger
jokes.punchline: Lisa suggests fatty foods instead, Homer gets angry about anger suggestion
jokes.explanation: Classic irony of Homer getting angry while trying to give up anger. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: If you ask me, you should give up fatty foods. I said anger!
jokes.setup: Homer vowing to give up anger
jokes.punchline: Marge suggests diet, Homer gets angry correcting her
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - gets angry while discussing giving up anger

jokes.text: God said to Noah build yourself an arky-arky / Animals came on by onesies and twosies-twosies / Elephants and kangaroosies-roosies
jokes.setup: Ned leading choir practice in Homer's house
jokes.punchline: Extremely cutesy version of biblical story with made-up rhymes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders character moment with infantile religious language. Cringe comedy through excessive cuteness.

jokes.text: Looks like the bubble burst. Bubbles can burst? Yeah, but it's a golden age for the repo business. One that shall never end.
jokes.setup: Bart's innocent question about economic bubbles
jokes.punchline: Repo man's ominous economic prediction delivered cheerfully
jokes.explanation: Good economic satire with dark humor about predatory industries benefiting from others' failures.

jokes.text: What's 52 million times zero? And don't tell me it's zero!
jokes.setup: Bart learning his massive stock holdings are worthless
jokes.punchline: Desperate plea to deny basic mathematics
jokes.explanation: Good desperation comedy showing Bart's denial of mathematical reality. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: Dirty diapers on strings!
jokes.setup: Homer continuing through trap
jokes.punchline: Encounters hanging dirty diapers
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor - dirty diapers as trap element

jokes.text: Homer mad! Homer smash! Get revenge on world!
jokes.setup: All the anger traps finally trigger Homer's rage
jokes.punchline: Homer transforms into Hulk-like primitive speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to extended setup. Hulk speech pattern works well for Homer's regression to pure anger.

jokes.text: Thank God his pants stayed on.
jokes.setup: Homer transforms into rage monster
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical concern about maintaining decency during rampage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge reaction - finding the silver lining in a disaster. Brilliant character-specific humor with excellent economy.

jokes.text: He can't be the Hulk! I'm the Hulk! Please. You couldn't even change into Bill Bixby.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy claiming to be the Hulk when Homer rampages
jokes.punchline: Stan Lee's brutal putdown referencing the TV show actor
jokes.explanation: Excellent comic book reference humor with Stan Lee delivering the perfect insult about Comic Book Guy's physique vs. Bill Bixby.

jokes.text: Ha-ha! You love a boy! Stop it now! If you love me so much, why don't you marry me? That's a good one.
jokes.setup: Homer expressing affection for Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart using childhood taunts to get Homer angry again
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart using playground psychology to manipulate Homer's emotions.
